hints.text: Gen3 unique car: **roof rack w/ black tape on the right front end** + visible hood wrap w/ a **red rectangle** — usually all you need for Ghana
Gen4 = **grey/white pickup w/ a front antenna**, mostly fully or partly blurred
Plates: **white private / yellow commercial**, long or short — commercial traffic is so common you'll see both constantly

shared_with.note: Nigerian white plates show a GREEN tint through the blur.

hints.text: Mostly **flat w/ red soil**; tropical vegetation, palms esp. toward the south
Taxis w/ **multicoloured body panels** — fenders & quarter panels always **orange-yellow**
Road lines always **white** — many paved roads have none at all
Typically **wooden poles w/ 3 insulators on a metal crossbar**

shared_with.diff: Ghana tape car → Ghana; follow SUV → Kenya.

hints.text: Patriotic paint everywhere: **red/yellow/green + black star** (flag colours)
hints.uniq_note: The flag-colour painting habit is a strong Ghana vibe.

hints.text: **B&W striped signposts** in cities; plain metal posts rural (striped posts exist elsewhere — combine clues)
Coverage limited & memorable: main roads + big cities, densest around **Accra & Kumasi** (Gen4 mostly near those two); paved/unpaved distribution map
**Tape meta** (region decider!): 'unpeeled tape' → southwest; 'peeled tape' → east + north; 'scotched tape' (2 white spots left) → north of Kumasi + west of Tamale
North = **savanna** (dry soil, sparse vegetation); tall mountain ranges → around Lake Volta + near Togo; dense forests + wild palms → southwest & east; tall coastal palms → mostly west

**Road spotlights**: unpaved dry-savanna roads → north of Wa; Sawla↔N10 = crisp new lines, E-W, lush savanna; **Salaga↔Tamale (to Kpalbusi)** = dirt road w/ moody overcast + dark purple-brown soil; Nkwanta↔Domanko = unpaved SE-NW w/ a powerline east; N2 east of Lake Volta = ridgeline SE + grey overcast; N1 east of Accra = flat, open, sparse; N1 near Elubo = brand-new dark asphalt w/ crisp middle line, no outers; Keta Lagoon road = sandy coastal + grey rocks

**Rare cars**: nature reserves = white car w/ visible mirrors (tape still there); Ankasa Game Reserve = whole front visible, roof vinyl + tape; Boabeng-Fiema Monkey Sanctuary = no tape/rack, black right mirror just visible
